instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertions on What is Covered under a Critical Illness Insurance Policy? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: critical illness cover, critical illness insurance, critical illness policy
Body Content:
Introduction
A critical illness cover has become essential in recent years, as incidences of lifestyle-related diseases rise exponentially. In India alone, non-communicable diseases (NCDs) are on a rise, with NCDs accounting for 61% deaths in 2016 compared to 38% in 1990. These findings by the health journal Lancet suggest that the incidences of NCDs, which include diabetes, cancer, heart diseases, and chronic kidney diseases, among others, are expected to continue rising over the years.
As more and more people become subject to such illnesses, the need to combat them is also growing significantly. Critical illness insurance policies have become more important than ever as they help with a range of expenditures needed for treatment.
What is Covered Under a Critical Illness Insurance Policy
A critical illness policy ensures that if faced with a critical illness, the family does not have to dip into their savings for treatment. There are several situations in which  a critical illness cover offers relief. Read on to learn more.
A critical illness cover offers a lump sum payment of the sum assured at the time the illness is detected.
While a traditional health insurance policy only covers expenditure related to hospitalisation, a critical illness insurance plan goes much beyond. It covers the cost of medication, treatment, doctor consultations, post-surgery, therapy, and even at-home care.
Additionally, a critical illness policy will also cover other expenditure not directly related to the treatment. For instance, if the breadwinner in a family contracts a critical illness and is unable to go to work, thereby losing out on a regular income, other expenses are bound to pile up, such as home loan EMIs or children’s school fees. Critical illness cover helps meet these household expenses as well.
FAQs
Why should I purchase a critical illness insurance cover if I already have a health insurance policy?While a health insurance policy will help ease the costs of hospitalisation and treatment to some degree, a critical illness insurance cover will help you cover the full cost of treatment, medication, recuperation aids, and even the cost of travelling to a different city for treatment. Additionally, it can help pay off debts and operate as a fund that helps compensate income lost due to illness and any changes in lifestyle.
Do I have to undergo medical tests before availing a critical illness insurance plan?Most insurers do not require you to undergo medical tests before availing a critical illness insurance plan. However, the eligibility criteria for insurers may vary and your premiums may be based on the current status of the policyholder’s health.
How many times can a policyholder raise claims on a critical illness cover?Regardless for whether it is for the same critical illness or others, a policyholder can only raise a claim once during the policy’s lifetime.
Are pre-existing critical conditions covered under the critical illness insurance cover?Pre-existing critical conditions refer to the critical illnesses a policyholder may have contracted before availing this policy. These conditions cannot be covered under the policy since the policyholder was already suffering from them before availing the policy.
Are there tax benefits for availing critical illness insurance? Yes, tax benefits are available under Section 80D of the Income Tax Act for premiums paid towards this insurance policy, among other health insurance policies.
Conclusion
A critical illness policy can help the policyholder meet a range of expenses if they get diagnosed with any critical illness. It is an important addition to the traditional health insurance policy since it also helps the policyholder supplement the income, they lose due to contracting the illness and subsequent treatment.
References:
https://securenow.in/insuropedia/what-is-covered-under-a-critical-illness-insurance-policy/
https://www.hdfcergo.com/faqs/critical-illness-insurance-policy-faqs
https://www.sbicard.com/en/faq/critical-illness-insurance-policy.page
